behaviorsAudited: exposes agent spawn, send, wait, list, close, and task-board operations through the tool surface without bypassing AgentControl
forks parent context into child prompts with preserved tool-use placeholders and per-child instructions
reports partial progress, final output, and failure state in task-compatible output records
keeps background task lifecycle, agent lifecycle, and TUI task views synchronized from one authoritative state
applies role allowlists and tool policies before child tools execute

spawn_agent v2 accepts service_tier, validates it against the effective child model, applies role model/reasoning/service_tier after valid request overrides, allows explicit service_tier with full-history forks, and forwards the selected tier to child runs

spawn_agent v2 deliberately diverges from the pinned source full-history default: omitted fork_turns is a clean fork, the schema documents that AgenC default, and explicit fork_turns all retains inherited role/model/reasoning settings
spawn_agent v2 permits a child agent to spawn descendants under its current agent path and applies a per-call child depth allowance instead of rejecting on the session max-depth cap
assign_task v2 rejects self, peer, busy, completed, and already-reserved targets; a strict ancestor may atomically admit one correlated task only to an idle reusable worker
accepted reusable-worker tasks durably record one correlated completed, errored, interrupted, or NACK outcome in the child journal before the parent receipt is projected
nested agent identity is accepted only from a valid signed live-session credential pair and rejects missing halves, forged signatures, and unknown sessions without root fallback
isolated child worktree results expose immutable integration evidence only for stable committed-clean outcomes and fail closed for dirty, diverged, unstable, or unverifiable states
send_message, assign_task, and close_agent v2 register the current root thread before resolving id or path targets so canonical /root maps to the active root session
list_agents v2 registers the current root thread before listing so the root entry is present even before any child spawn has registered it
wait_agent v2 waits for a parent mailbox update, drains delivered mailbox records into optional updates, and returns message plus timed_out
wait_agent v2 validates, defaults, and describes timeout_ms from configured min, default, and max wait timeout options
close_agent emits the close-end event with the previous status even when close request delivery fails
spawn_agents_on_csv exposes the Rust CSV agent job schema including max_workers as a max_concurrency alias and dispatches worker subagents through the same background delegate path
report_agent_job_result records worker result objects and stop requests into the active CSV agent job state

edgeCasesAudited: wait_agent returns completed when any parent mailbox update arrives before the timeout
wait_agent returns timed_out when no parent mailbox update arrives before the timeout
wait_agent rejects timeout_ms below the configured minimum instead of silently clamping
wait_agent uses the configured default timeout when timeout_ms is omitted
wait_agent rejects timeout_ms above the configured maximum instead of using the global maximum
wait_agent rejects fractional timeout_ms values instead of passing fractional milliseconds into the mailbox wait
wait_agent rejects removed v1 targets input as an unknown field
spawn_agent rejects an explicit service_tier unsupported by the role-resolved child model before delegation
spawn_agent gives role service_tier precedence over a valid requested service_tier when the resolved model supports both
spawn_agent with omitted fork_turns dispatches a clean fork without a forkMode and exposes a schema description that documents the clean-fork default
spawn_agent with omitted fork_turns permits validated reasoning/model/role overrides because the default is a clean fork
spawn_agent with explicit fork_turns none allows role and reasoning overrides and dispatches without a forkMode
spawn_agent allows an explicit service_tier when fork_turns is all while still rejecting full-history model and effort overrides
spawn_agent called from a depth-one child with agent_max_depth set to one spawns a grandchild under the child path and emits begin/end lifecycle events
list_agents called before any child spawn on an unregistered AgentControl returns the registered /root entry instead of an empty agent list
send_message called from a child to /root on an unregistered AgentControl queues an upward non-triggering inter-agent communication to the root session
assign_task called from a child to /root on an unregistered AgentControl returns the root-task rejection without enqueuing root mail
close_agent called with /root on an unregistered AgentControl returns the root-not-spawned rejection instead of an unresolved path error
a child spawned with service_tier priority forwards priority into the provider request options
child session tool filtering keeps v2 agent tools visible at the configured depth cap while still blocking main-thread coordination tools
assign_task returns task_id and turn_id for an idle child, then rejects a racing second assignment until the first reaches a terminal receipt
an assignment accepted immediately before teardown is NACKed with its original task_id and turn_id without a duplicate generic completion
passive peer context remains in the bounded mailbox until an assignment and is framed with its authenticated author as untrusted input
a missing or forged nested identity credential returns invalid-runtime-identity while root fallback remains available only when both credential fields are absent
a fresh automatic worktree name changes across repeated spawns and committed-clean evidence is withheld when either status or HEAD changes during verification
closing a running agent interrupts it once and produces an idempotent terminal task status
a close_agent shutdown failure still emits collab_close_end and returns a structured tool error
a fork containing multiple pending tool calls creates one placeholder result per call before child instructions
an agent tool with a disallowed role fails before any child run is started
task-board output excludes internal mailbox details while preserving user-visible progress and status
max_workers set to one runs a two-row CSV agent job sequentially instead of dispatching both workers concurrently
max_concurrency set to zero normalizes to one CSV worker instead of falling back to the default concurrency
a worker report with stop true cancels the remaining CSV rows while preserving the completed worker result

commandResults: PASS: 16 test files passed, 331 tests passed
implementationEvidence: The v2 tool surface delegates spawn, passive message, correlated assignment, wait, list, and close operations through AgentControl; the deleted followup_task alias is not callable.
spawn_agent uses a documented clean-fork default, keeps explicit all as the full-history opt-in, validates model, reasoning, and service-tier combinations, and preserves role precedence.
AgentControl admits one correlated assignment only to an idle descendant, and runAgent records one canonical child-journal outcome before attempting parent projection.

currentAgentContext accepts nested identity only through a valid HMAC-signed session-id pair for the root or a known live child; incomplete, forged, mismatched, and unknown credentials return invalid-runtime-identity.
wait_agent drains delivered mailbox records into optional updates and enforces integer minimum, default, and maximum timeout policy.

Automatic worktree identities include session, full agent path, and per-spawn identity; evidence capture exposes integrationRef only for a stable committed-clean descendant and fails closed for dirty, diverged, unstable, or unverifiable states.
CSV job tools retain bounded concurrency aliases, sequential normalization, worker result reporting, and stop semantics.
